product_segments.name: Commercial (Foundry/AIP)
product_segments.source: Calculated from total revenue minus government segment; also U.S. Commercial $595M per investor presentation

management_guidance.context: Raised from prior guidance, representing 71% YoY growth
Confirmed expectation of GAAP profitability in every quarter

competitive_position.moat: Strong government contracts, 'land and expand' model with AIP platform, high switching costs from deep integration
competitive_position.threats: Intense competition from large tech firms with comparable AI capabilities and higher operating margins

key_facts.fact: Palantir raised its 2026 revenue guidance to $7.656 billion, up 71% YoY
U.S. commercial revenue guidance raised to over $3.224 billion, growth at least 120%
Q1 2026 revenue was $1.6326 billion, up 85% YoY
U.S. commercial revenue in Q1 2026 was $595 million, up 133% YoY
U.S. government revenue in Q1 2026 was $687 million, up 84% YoY

key_facts.caveat: Calculated as revenue growth + operating margin; not a standard accounting metric

key_facts.fact: Eleven consecutive quarters of accelerating revenue growth

analyst_thesis.bull_case: Palantir is delivering ~70% revenue growth, ~80% EPS growth, 41% operating margin, and ~$760M in quarterly free cash flow, driven by triple-digit commercial revenue growth from AIP platform and land-and-expand model.
analyst_thesis.bear_case: High valuation compared to peers, lower operating margins than Microsoft/Google, and intense competition in the AI data analytics space pose risks.

key_institutions.thesis: Average target implies 12.82% upside from current price; mix of 5 Buys, 10 Holds, 2 Sells

data_quality.notes: Segment data from 10-Q and investor presentation; guidance from earnings transcript and press release; EDGAR XBRL provided revenue history; no ARR or bookings data found.
